text: The advancement of parallel convolution technologies has significantly enhanced the complexity and functionality of optical neural networks (ONNs) by leveraging the wavelength dimension. Despite these gains, high-level integration and efficient on-chip operation remain formidable challenges. In this work, we demonstrate a microcomb-driven photonic processing unit (PPU) that implements convolution using a time-wavelength plane stretching approach. By developing a dedicated control and operation protocol to support the PPU, we achieve a record-breaking weight precision of 9 bits. The system's compact architecture and rapid data-loading capabilities enable a landmark photonic-core compute density exceeding 1 trillion operations per second per square millimeter (TOPS mm⁻²). We validate the platform through two proof-of-concept experiments—image edge detection and handwritten digit recognition—attaining performance levels comparable to digital electronic computers. Given its superior performance and inherent scalability, this parallel photonic processing unit offers a transformative path for demanding artificial intelligence tasks, such as autonomous driving, real-time video action recognition, and high-fidelity image reconstruction.
